metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_2238
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Background: Authors show that kalirin protein locates to the dendritic spine to control actin dynamics. In a previous publication (PMID:10692441) the authors show that KALRN is enriched in the PSD fraction.

Fig 2: "CaMKII interacts with and phosphorylates kalirin-7"
"CaMKII coimmunoprecipitated with kalirin-7 and PSD-95 from rat forebrain homogenates (Figure 2A), suggesting that these proteins participate in the same multiprotein complex"

Fig 3: Confocal microscopy of cortical pyramidal neurons show strong localisation of kalirin-7 at the tips of spines indicating specific localisation at the PSD.

Fig 4: Competition with C-terminal Kalirin 7 peptide (competitor of interaction with PSD95) prevents Kalirin accumulation at dendritic spines. Experimental description: - Dissociated culture: "Medium and high density cortical neuron cultures were prepared from Sprague-Dawley rat
E18 embryos as described previously (Xie et al., 2005)."
- Overexpressed KALRN is of rat species (traced back to PMID:10692441): "Rat cerebral cortex cDNA was generated by reverse transcription of poly(A)+ mRNA with an oligo-(dT)15 primer (Promega). A 900-bp PCR product was generated by amplification with a Kalirin-8 sense primer (nt 4071-4092) and an antisense primer 5'-GGTCTAGACGGCTAACCCGGTCAGCT-3' (rat nt 4978-4960)-basedHomo sapiens EST T74341, which included an XbaI site (underlined). The PCR product was subcloned and sequenced. A cDNA encoding full-length Kalirin-7 was constructed by replacing the 3'-end of Kalirin-8 (with the P-CIP10a 5'-end (2)) with the 3'-end of Kalirin-7"
